column1: # Phase 4a catalog of every legacy alias #define'd in the four pre-SSOT
# compat sources. Input to gen_errors_compat.py. Each row lists the legacy
# spelling, where it currently lives, and the canonical errors.tsv name
# the generator will point the alias at post-cutover.
# This file is an INPUT to the generator (like errors.tsv), not an output.
# It is hand-maintained; only edit when mining a new legacy source or when
# a human has approved a semantic re-targeting.
# Rows preceded by "# CHANGED:" flag an intentional mapping change from
# the pre-SSOT target. Reason is appended to the comment line.
# Symbols that are now CANONICAL enum members in data/errors.tsv
# FILE_OPEN, FILE_READ, FILE_WRITE, FILE_SEEK, FORMAT_INVALID, FILE_CREATE,
# PLUGIN_LOAD, PLUGIN_NOT_FOUND) are NOT listed below — their UFT_ERR_
# spelling is the enum name, and the UFT_ERROR_* mixed-case spelling is
# emitted by the main compat loop in gen_errors_compat.py.
# current_target — what the pre-SSOT header expands to (enum member,
# source_file   — short tag: err_h | compat_h | codes_h | unified_h
# proposed_canonical — name from data/errors.tsv that the generator
# === UFT_ERR_* legacy aliases (do NOT become canonical enum members) =====
# These are pure alias spellings. Nothing in here is a new enum value.
# CHANGED: UFT_ERR_NULL_PTR was → INVALID_ARG; NULL_POINTER exists as distinct canonical (86 call sites) so retarget for better diagnostics.
# CHANGED: UFT_ERC_FORMAT is a typo spelling of UFT_ERR_FORMAT; compat had it mapped to CORRUPT, but the non-typo canonical is FORMAT. Retarget.

# CHANGED: UFT_ERR_NO_DATA was → CORRUPTED (unified_h) / FORMAT (UFT_ERROR_NO_DATA in err_h). Approved semantic fix: MISSING_SECTOR reflects the "sector header says data-of-length-N but no data found" forensics meaning.
# === UFT_ERROR_* mixed-case legacy aliases (in err_h and unified_h) =====
# These are the "noisy" 1127-site spellings. Each row maps to a canonical.
# The main compat loop auto-generates UFT_ERROR_<NAME> for every canonical
# row in errors.tsv, so only the aliases that have NO matching canonical
# CHANGED: UFT_ERROR_NULL_POINTER was → INVALID_ARG; retargeted to distinct NULL_POINTER canonical for consistency with UFT_ERR_NULL_PTR.
# CHANGED: UFT_ERROR_FILE_OPEN was → IO; FILE_OPEN now canonical (code -15), so retarget for specificity.
# CHANGED: UFT_ERROR_DISK_PROTECTED was → NOT_PERMITTED; WRITE_PROTECT now canonical (code -54), more specific.
# CHANGED: UFT_ERROR_TRACK_NOT_FOUND was → FORMAT; MISSING_SECTOR is forensically closer (track header present, data missing).
# CHANGED: UFT_ERROR_INVALID_STATE was → INVALID_ARG; INVALID_STATE now canonical (code -93).
# CHANGED: UFT_ERROR_CANCELLED was → TIMEOUT; CANCELLED now canonical (code -92).
# CHANGED: UFT_ERROR_TRANSACTION_CONFLICT was → INVALID_ARG; BUSY is semantically correct.
# CHANGED: UFT_ERROR_TRANSACTION_LIMIT was → INTERNAL; RESOURCE (limit exceeded) is semantically correct.
# CHANGED: was IO; FILE_WRITE now canonical (code -19).
# CHANGED: was IO; FILE_READ now canonical (code -16).
# CHANGED: was FORMAT; ENCODING now canonical (code -27).
# CHANGED: was INTERNAL; PLUGIN_LOAD now canonical (code -95).
# CHANGED: was NOT_SUPPORTED; PLUGIN_NOT_FOUND now canonical (code -96).
# CHANGED: was IO; FILE_SEEK now canonical (code -18).
# CHANGED: was FORMAT; UNKNOWN_FORMAT now canonical (code -26), more specific.
# CHANGED: approved semantic fix — was FORMAT, now distinct FORMAT_INVALID (code -25).
# CHANGED: was FORMAT; bad-header is a structural invalidity → FORMAT_INVALID.
# CHANGED: was FORMAT; decode failure is an encoding-layer issue.
# CHANGED: was TIMEOUT; BUSY is semantically correct.
# CHANGED: was TIMEOUT; BUSY is semantically correct.
# CHANGED: was CRC; WEAK_BITS is the correct forensic semantic (fuzzy bits = weak bits).
# CHANGED: was IO; no-disk-in-drive is closer to DEVICE_NOT_FOUND.
# CHANGED: was FORMAT; no index pulse means no sync acquired.
# CHANGED: was FORMAT; SYNC_LOST now canonical (code -60).
# CHANGED: was FORMAT; PLL_UNLOCK now canonical (code -67).
# CHANGED: was INTERNAL; PLUGIN_LOAD now canonical (code -95).
# CHANGED: was INTERNAL; VERSION now canonical (code -94).
# CHANGED: was IO; FILE_SEEK now canonical (code -18).
# CHANGED: was FORMAT; unformatted disk has structural invalidity.
# CHANGED: was CRC; VERIFY_FAIL now canonical (code -4).
# CHANGED: was CRC; WEAK_BITS now canonical (code -61).
# CHANGED: was IO; disk-not-open is an invalid-state condition.
# CHANGED: was FORMAT; decode is encoding-layer.
# CHANGED: was IO; EOF now canonical (code -14), more specific.
# (Generated by existing SHORT_E_ALIASES loop; listed here for coverage.)
# CHANGED: was CORRUPT; FORMAT is the semantically correct target.
# === UFT_E_* subsystem codes (codes_h — the P2-ARCH-006 surface) ==========
# These are a parallel numeric space (-1..-799) that the codes_h header
# defined. Post-SSOT, each maps to the closest canonical enum member.
# Many of them duplicate UFT_ERR_<NAME> with only the prefix changed.
# === UFT_IR_* / UFT_DEC_* subsystem aliases (codes_h legacy sections) =====
